headline: AI-assisted development broke out this month as Claude Fable 5 and large-scale coding agents dominated the field.
body: AI-assisted development was the clear breakout, improving with momentum 145, anchored by Claude Fable 5 tutorials, coding-agent orchestration, and workflow automation pipelines [1][2][8]. Cursor detailed running 128 coding agents at once with inter-agent communication and review workflows, pushing multi-agent orchestration and parallel execution to the foreground [10]. Claude Fable 5 also drew serious backlash over aggressive safety filters that refuse harmless prompts and raise fallback-model and guardrail trust questions [4][5]. On the infrastructure side, KV cache quantization broke out and ASIC accelerators entered as a new technique, while privacy-preserving inference and encrypted reasoning lagged despite high ratios.
